3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: The purpose of the UE Context Modification procedure is to modify the established UE Context, e.g., establishing, modifying and releasing radio resources or sidelink resources. This procedure is also used to command the gNB-DU to stop data transmission for the UE for mobility (see TS 38.401 [4]).
This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves within the same gNB-DU during NR operation for LTM (Intra-gNB-DU LTM).
This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (Inter-gNB-DU LTM).
Feature_Objectives: To enable the gNB-CU to initiate LTM configuration.
To allow the gNB-DU to accept LTM configuration requests and provide generated lower layer RRC configurations.
To facilitate early synchronization and cell switch during LTM handover.
To manage the release of configured candidate cells after LTM handover.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. L3 Measurement Control and Reports: The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. LTM Configuration Decision: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (LTM Configuration): The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may request the gNB-DU to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer reference configuration to the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may inform the gNB-DU about intra-DU L2 reset configuration.

4. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE (LTM Configuration): If the gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted candidate cell.
5. DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER (RRCReconfiguration): The gNB-CU sends a DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message to the gNB-DU, which includes the generated RRCReconfiguration message with the LTM configuration.
6. RRCReconfiguration: The gNB-DU forwards the received RRCReconfiguration message to the UE.
7. RRCReconfigurationComplete: The UE responds to the gNB-DU with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message.
8. UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER (RRCReconfigurationComplete): The gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-CU.

9. CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST: If LTM is triggered based on L3 measurement, gNB-CU-CP sends a CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU to trigger early synchronization and/or cell switch.
10. Early TA acquisition: The UE performs early TA acquisition.
11. L1 measurement report: The UE sends the lower layer measurement result to the source gNB-DU if L1 measurement is configured to UE.
12. LTM Cell Switch Decision: The gNB-DU decides to execute LTM to a target cell.
13. Cell Switch Command: The gNB-DU sends the Cell Switch Command to the UE.
14. DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION: The gNB-DU sends the DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION message to the gNB-CU with the selected target cell ID.
15. UE Access Detection: The gNB-DU detects the UE access.
16. ACCESS SUCCESS: The gNB-DU sends an ACCESS SUCCESS message to the gNB-CU with the target cell ID.
17. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (Release Prepared Cells): The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU to release prepared cells.
18. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE: The gNB-DU responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. L3 Measurement Control and Reports: The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. LTM Configuration Decision: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (LTM Configuration): The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may request the gNB-DU to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer reference configuration to the gNB-DU. The gNB-CU may inform the gNB-DU about intra-DU L2 reset configuration.

4. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE (LTM Configuration): If the gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted candidate cell.
5. DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER (RRCReconfiguration): The gNB-CU sends a DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message to the gNB-DU, which includes the generated RRCReconfiguration message with the LTM configuration.
6. RRCReconfiguration: The gNB-DU forwards the received RRCReconfiguration message to the UE.
7. RRCReconfigurationComplete: The UE responds to the gNB-DU with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message.
8. UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER (RRCReconfigurationComplete): The gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-CU.

9. CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST: If LTM is triggered based on L3 measurement, gNB-CU-CP sends a CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU to trigger early synchronization and/or cell switch.
10. Early TA acquisition: The UE performs early TA acquisition.
11. L1 measurement report: The UE sends the lower layer measurement result to the source gNB-DU if L1 measurement is configured to UE.
12. LTM Cell Switch Decision: The gNB-DU decides to execute LTM to a target cell.
13. Cell Switch Command: The gNB-DU sends the Cell Switch Command to the UE.
14. DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION: The gNB-DU sends the DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION message to the gNB-CU with the selected target cell ID.
15. UE Access Detection: The gNB-DU detects the UE access.
16. ACCESS SUCCESS: The gNB-DU sends an ACCESS SUCCESS message to the gNB-CU with the target cell ID.
17. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (Release Prepared Cells): The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message to the gNB-DU to release prepared cells.
18. UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE: The gNB-DU responds with a UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition: Main message IE definition for UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST. Contains various IEs for modifying UE context, including LTM-specific information such as LTM Information Modify IE, SpCell ID IE, LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE, Early Sync Information Request IE, Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE, Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE, LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE, LTM Cells to be Released List IE, LTM Reset Information IE, and CSI Resource Configuration IE.

Main message IE definition for UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE. Contains various IEs reporting the outcome of UE context modification, including LTM-specific information such as Requested Target Cell ID IE, LTM Configuration IE, and Early Sync Information IE.

If the LTM Indicator IE set to 'true' is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE and shall include it as the Requested Target Cell ID IE in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message. The gNB-DU shall regard it as a reconfiguration with sync as defined in TS 38.331 [8]. If the gNB-DU accepts the request for LTM for that SpCell, the gNB-DU shall generate and include the CellGroupConfig IE for the accepted LTM candidate cell in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message. Also contains Reference Configuration IE and CSI Resource Configuration IE.

If the SpCell ID IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message and neither the LTM Information Modify IE nor the Conditional Intra-DU Mobility Information IE is present, the gNB-DU shall replace any previously received value and regard it as a reconfiguration with sync as defined in TS 38.331 [8]. When included with LTM Information Modify IE, it indicates the SpCell for which LTM is requested.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, consider this as the mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: If the Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it as specified in TS 38.401 [4]. If the UE Based TA Measurement Configuration IE is contained in the Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE for some candidate cell, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take them into account for UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch as specified in TS 38.331 [8].

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: If the Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it as specified in TS 38.401 [4]. If the UE Based TA Measurement Configuration IE is contained in the Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take it into account for UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch as specified in TS 38.331 [8].

Information_Elements.IE_Name: LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: If the LTM CFRA Resource Config List IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it for the LTM cell switch command as specified in TS 38.321 [16].

Information_Elements.IE_Name: LTM Cells to be Released List IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: If the LTM Cells to be Released List IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, release the configured candidate cells in the list.

If the LTM Reset Information IE is contained in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, take them into account for L2 reset (i.e., RLC re-establishment) during an intra-DU LTM cell switch as specified in TS38.331 [8].

If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message and the SpCell ID IE is also included, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell identified by the SpCell ID IE. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Modify IE included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message while the SpCell ID IE is absent, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration in the CellGroupConfig IE for the serving cell.
When LTM is requested for an SpCell ID IE in UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST, the gNB-DU shall include it as the Requested Target Cell ID IE in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message.

If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell in the gNB-DU. Contains Reference Configuration Information IE and Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE.

If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall, if supported, include Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message. Contains Early UL Sync Configuration IE and Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE.
Contained in LTM Information Modify IE. When set to 'true', indicates the request concerns LTM.
Contained in LTM Information Modify IE. Contains Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE and Reference Configuration Information IE.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: Contained within Reference Configuration IE in LTM Information Modify IE. If set to 'true', the gNB-DU shall provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE message for the gNB-CU to generate the LTM reference configuration.

Contained within Reference Configuration IE in LTM Information Modify IE or LTM Configuration IE. If contained in LTM Information Modify IE, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration. If contained in LTM Configuration IE, it provides lower layer configuration for the gNB-CU to generate the LTM reference configuration.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: UE Based TA Measurement Configuration IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: Contained in Early Sync Candidate Cell Information List IE or Early Sync Serving Cell Information IE. The gNB-DU shall take it into account for UE based TA measurement during LTM cell switch as specified in TS 38.331 [8].
Contained in LTM Configuration IE. If set to 'complete', the gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.
Contained in Early Sync Information IE. The gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated early UL sync information from the accepted candidate cell in the gNB-DU.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE
Information_Elements.IE_Definition: Contained in Early Sync Information IE. The gNB-CU shall, if supported, consider it as the generated early UL sync information for SUL from the accepted candidate cell in the gNB-DU.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The RRCReconfiguration message, containing the LTM configuration, is piggy-backed within the DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.
